text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl named Oliver.

Every day, he would flap his wings, whistle a sweet song, and survey the vast kingdom over his forest hill.

One particular autumn day, while roasting walnuts by the firelight, Oliver fell into a deep reverie.

He imagined a land far away where a peculiar creature lived.

This creature had two hearts.

One would soar with the eagles.

The other would beat steadily like a well-timed drum.

And yet, somehow, that rhythm joined the eagles' flight—it soared even higher.

One day, the eagle tired.

The drumming faltered.

The birds began to doubt.

But, Oliver the wise owl swooped down.

He whispered secrets of patience.

He showed them how to soar gracefully even when steady beats seem to weigh them down.

Together they soared once more into the vast, mysterious sky.

As dawn rose, the eagles lifted off, leaving behind only a gentle breeze humming Oliver's
